humanities_role_explanation: Designed specifically for evaluating expert-level historical knowledge and comprehension using curated historical data from the Seshat Global History Databank
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Text-based evaluation using structured historical data points converted into multiple-choice questions covering global historical societies and periods
analytical_tasks_explanation: Expert-level historical knowledge evaluation through four-choice format questions based on curated historical data covering 600 historical societies from Neolithic to Industrial Revolution periods across major world regions

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Evaluates general historical knowledge capabilities across multiple LLM families

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluated models from Gemini, OpenAI (GPT-4-Turbo), and Llama (Llama-3.1-8B) families

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for historical knowledge comprehension

arts_humanities_data.note: History - uses Seshat Global History Databank with 36,000 data points across historical societies and scholarly references

quantitative_metrics.note: Balanced accuracy scores ranging from 33.6% to 46% compared to random baseline of 25%

english_language.note: Paper and benchmark appear to be in English

summary: HiST-LLM introduces a benchmark for evaluating LLMs' expert-level historical knowledge using the Seshat Global History Databank, covering 36,000 data points across 600 historical societies from the Neolithic to Industrial Revolution. The benchmark tests seven models from major LLM families using a four-choice format, finding performance ranges from 33.6% to 46% accuracy.
key_findings: LLMs achieved balanced accuracy between 33.6% (Llama-3.1-8B) and 46% (GPT-4-Turbo), outperforming random chance (25%) but falling short of expert-level comprehension. Models performed better on earlier historical periods, with regional performance being more balanced but lowest in Oceania and Sub-Saharan Africa.
